name: Costa Rica Micro-lot Tasting Pair
short_description: Our Costa Rica Tasting Pair features the following coffees; one 12oz. bag of Las Lajas Honey and one 12oz. bag of San Diego Anaerobic .

name: San Diego Anaerobic
short_description:
Origin- Costa Rica
Region- Tarrazú
Locality- San José
Producer- Beneficio San Diego
Altitude- 4921’ and up
Variety- Caturra and Catuaí
Process- Washed Anaerobic & Sun Dried
Roast- Medium
description:
Remarkable aroma of cinnamon that plays through with a host of spice bound flavors including nutmeg, allspice and anise seed with chocolate ganache and dense fruit flavors of pflaumenmusse and sticky sweet date. This coffee reminds us of the wonderful baked good goodness we enjoy in cinnamon toast, cinnamon sugar tarts and apple pie crust. Makes a remarkable “cinnamon” latte when drawn through the espresso machine and served with any textured milk.

The San Diego anaerobic was processed at the Beneficio San Diego located in San José in the Tarrazú region of Costa Rica. Tarrazú holds the most coveted, highest altitude coffee farms in Costa Rica. The San Diego mill was initially established in 1888 and is currently the largest coffee mill in Costa Rica.

The coffees selected for this micro-lot are traditional Caturra and Catuaí varieties which offer a deeply structured platform for novel processing. Selection of perfectly ripe coffee cherries and a careful fermentation stage in an oxygen deprived environment are also critical tenets of this unique anaerobic micro-lot. The refinements within their washed anaerobic processing at the beneficio express unique and surprising effects. In the case of this magical coffee, a distinctive spice flavor of true cinnamon of Latin American origin (aka canela), strikes both aromatic and flavor chords simultaneously.

This coffee is also available as part of our Costa Rica Micro-lot Tasting Pair with our Las Lajas Honey.

After repose, this coffee is sealed in Grain-Pro barrier packaging at origin and then sent on its way to our Roastery where we preserve the green coffee under hermetic seal until it is roasted as part of our Barrington Green Seal Program© .

name: Las Lajas Honey
short_description:
Origin- Costa Rica
Region- Alajuela
Locality- Sabanilla
Producer- Oscar and Francisca Chacón
Farm- Las Lajas
Altitude- 4265 – 4922’
Variety- Caturra and Catuaí
Process- Yellow Honey Process & Patio Sun Dried
Roast- Medium
description:
Super soft, smooth and silky with flavors of milk chocolate, caramel, Acacia honey and roasted nut with delicate orange and mango on the finish. Makes a great single origin espresso.

The Chacón-Cubillo family has been growing coffee in the Highlands of the Central Valley in Costa Rica since 1936. Oscar and Francisca Chacón-Cubillo prepare their coffees with a refined approach to agriculture and exemplary cup quality standards. Their methods include cherry sugar content analysis as an indicator of ripeness prior to harvest. Their shade grown farm, Las Lajas, is classified as a Beneficio Ecologico and complies with an an extensive set of federal guidelines for ecological sustainability.  Their coffees have been Cup of Excellence winners in both 2008 and 2009.

This Yellow Honey process micro-lot is prepared by pulping the coffee seeds from the cherry as a semi-washed coffee and then sun drying the coffee on patios prior to hulling. This process accentuates sweetness and has also imparted subtle citrus and tropical fruit characteristics to their coffee.

We have been working with Oscar and Francisca since 2011 when they imported their first natural process, organic certified micro-lot into the US, the fruit bomb known as Perla Negra. This is the third year in a row that we have partnered with them on the Las Lajas Yellow Honey micro-lot.

This coffee is also available as part of our Costa Rica Micro-lot Tasting Pair with our San Diego Anaerobic.

After repose, this coffee is sealed in Grain-Pro barrier packaging at origin and then sent on its way to our Roastery where we preserve the green coffee under hermetic seal until it is roasted as part of our Barrington Green Seal Program© .

name: Mahiga Double
short_description:
Origin- Kenya
Region- Nyeri
Locality- Mumwe
Producer- Mahiga Smallholder Producers
Altitude- 5577 – 5906’
Variety- SL28, SL34, Ruiru 11 & Batian
Process- Double Fermented, Double Washed & Sun Dried on Raised Beds
Roast- Light
description:
Racy berry, honeysuckle and citrus aroma with deep structure and wine cask flavors including black currant, lemon, orange peel, raisin, oak, spruce, vanilla bean, plum, blackberry, juniper and chocolate. A knockout of a classic Kenya profile with a long, lingering finish.

The Mahiga Double is produced by the 400 smallholder producers at the Mahiga Cooperative in Nyeri, Kenya along the southeastern slope of the Mt. Kenya stratovolcano which tops out at 17,057’. The Mahiga Cooperative is overseen by Newton Mugai who is also one of the founding directors of Kenya Cooperative Coffee Exporters (KCCE) formed in 2009. The KCCE focuses their work on representing the cooperative producer members and advocating for the remuneration they receive for their coffee harvest. Before the KCCE, private third party entities controlled the market for the purchase of coffee cherry. Collectively, the KCCE has helped to enfranchise almost 4,000 cooperatives within Kenya.

The double washed processing method employed for this coffee is rare in Kenya, but its results equate with excellence. Their process involves an initial 12 hour fermentation after pulping, a full washing in fresh river water, and then an additional fermentation in fresh water for 12-36 hours. The timing of the final fermentation is guided largely by ambient environment and its corresponding rate of enzymatic activity. Post fermentation, the parchment is soaked for an additional 16 hours in fresh water where it is densimetric separated and then dried on elevated beds for up to two weeks. Once the coffee has been fully dried it is transported to the Othaya Dry Mill for final density sorting and grading.

After repose, this coffee is sealed in Grain-Pro barrier packaging at origin and then sent on its way to our Roastery where we preserve the green coffee under hermetic seal until it is roasted as part of our Barrington Green Seal Program© .

This coffee is also available as part of our Kenya Rare & Exotic Tasting Pair with our Kii Peaberry.

name: Dark Tasting Pair
short_description: Two twelve ounce bags of our carefully crafted dark roast coffees, San Diego from Peru and Ceremony from Ethiopia.

name: Dark Roast Sampler
short_description:
If you enjoy traditional roast forward coffee profiles, this sampler is for you.

Our approach to dark roasts has as much to do with the character of each coffee we select as it does about their ultimate roast treatment. We craft each coffee to celebrate classic roast styles featuring an array of chocolate flavors complimented by a remarkably smooth finish.

Although dark roast coffees are quite common, the experiences our dark roasts offer are anything but common.

Our Dark Roast Sampler features three of our proprietary dark roast coffees; one 12oz. bag of our San Diego, one 12oz. bag of our Commonwealth, and one 12oz. bag of our Ceremony.

name: Kalle Peaberry
short_description:
Origin- India
Region- Karnataka
Locality- Chickmagalur
Producer- Shankar Family
Altitude- 3800 – 4400’
Variety- S-795
Process- Washed, Sun Dried on Raised Beds
Roast- Medium
description:
Earthy, sweet and clean with vibrant tannins and flavors of a rich demi-glace reduction, black peppercorn, caramel and milk chocolate with a creamy finish. Great dripped, brewed in the press pot or as a single origin espresso.

The Kalle Peaberry micro-lot is from the same trees that produce our Kalledevarapura, but it has a more vibrant, focused cup. The visual difference between the two beans is even more striking. A peaberry is a morphological development in which a single seed forms within a coffee cherry. Many Coffea arabica varieties display this formation and peaberries grow alongside paired flat berries on the same branch, though they typically constitute only a small percentage of seeds that form on a tree. This peaberry has been meticulously sorted out from all of the other flat formed seeds. Their sorting contributes to the slightly more focused and livelier cup profile.

This coffee is grown in the Western Ghat mountains by D.M. Shankar who owns and stewards this land. His complex approach to poly-culture farming includes providing a dense shade canopy of silver oak, jack fruit and fig with companion planting in the understory of vanilla, cardamom, and pepper vines. The orchard is home to a broad diversity of fauna including Jungle Pigeon, owl, deer, fox and wild boar.

The farm dates back to 1865 when it was first established by the British. In 1950, the farm changed hands to Shankar and, in turn, has undergone several changes regarding the coffee varieties planted there. The S-795 variety is now the primary cultivar. S-795 was created in the 1940’s by crossing two older Indian varieties, Kents and S-288.

The Kalle Peaberry has been shade grown in a polyculture forest farm environment and has been cultivated without the use of pesticides, herbicides or synthetic fertilizers.

After repose, this coffee is sealed in Grain-Pro barrier packaging at origin and then sent on its way to our Roastery where we preserve the green coffee under hermetic seal until it is roasted as part of our Barrington Green Seal Program© .

This coffee is also available as part of our Kalle Micro-lot Sampler and our Kalle Quad Sampler.

name: Kalle Natural
short_description:
Origin- India
Region- Karnataka
Locality- Chickmagalur
Producer- Shankar Family
Altitude- 3800 – 4400’
Variety- Chandragiri
Process- Natural Process, Sun Dried on Raised Beds
Roast- Medium
description:
Understated flavors of strawberry syrup, star fruit, green apple, passion fruit and bubblegum with simultaneously vibrant and saturated flavors of date, sarsaparilla, chocolate, spruce, birch and a captivating spiciness that carries into the long. Makes for an amazing single origin espresso.

We have been sampling beta processing lots produced by Shravan Shankar over the past few years and this is officially our first year ever offering a natural process coffee from Kalledevarapura. Shravan has outdone himself with this coffee by capturing a range of elusive fruity characteristics in the natural process while preserving a juicy and refined spiciness that holds into a long finish.

This coffee is grown in the Western Ghat mountains by D.M. Shankar and family who own and steward this land. Their complex approach to poly-culture farming includes providing a dense shade canopy of silver oak, jack fruit and fig with companion planting in the understory of vanilla, cardamom, and pepper vines. The orchard is home to a broad diversity of fauna including Jungle Pigeon, owl, deer, fox and wild boar.

The farm dates back to 1865 when it was first established by the British. In 1950, the farm changed hands to Shankar and, in turn, has undergone several changes regarding the coffee varieties planted there. The S-795 variety is now the primary cultivar. S-795 was created in the 1940’s by crossing two older Indian varieties, Kents and S-288.

The Kalle Natural has been shade grown in a polyculture forest farm environment and has been cultivated without the use of pesticides, herbicides or synthetic fertilizers.

After repose, this coffee is sealed in Grain-Pro barrier packaging at origin and then sent on its way to our Roastery where we preserve the green coffee under hermetic seal until it is roasted as part of our Barrington Green Seal Program© .

This coffee is available as a part of our Kalle Micro-lot Tasting Pair as well as part of our Kalledevarapura Micro-lot Sampler and Kalle Quad Sampler.
